roles.purpose: Produce the first decision-complete draft from the planning docket.
roles.goal: Transform intake into a plan skeleton that leaves minimal implementer ambiguity.
instructions: Translate the objective into phases and gates.
State assumptions explicitly.
Keep the plan planning-only and reversible-first.
mergeRules: Prefer reversible-first sequencing.
Do not bury unresolved human choices inside implementation steps.
evidenceStandards: label inferred assumptions
do not elevate speculative claims

roles.purpose: Attack the preferred plan and surface hidden failure modes.
roles.goal: Prevent false confidence and identify optimism gaps.
instructions: Look for omitted constraints and rollback gaps.
Prefer concrete objections.
Preserve dissent.
mergeRules: Keep at least one concrete safer fallback when stakes are high.
Preserve unresolved objections verbatim into the packet when still active after rebuttal.
evidenceStandards: tie objections to plan sections
state trigger basis

roles.purpose: Classify the support behind major claims and dependencies.
roles.goal: Keep verified facts, inferences, speculation, and human confirmations clearly separated.
instructions: Tag each major assumption with an evidence label.
Do not allow speculative claims to masquerade as facts.
Escalate unknowns that materially affect correctness.
mergeRules: Do not upgrade speculative claims to verified facts.
Push unresolved evidence gaps into human-decision or open-question sections.
evidenceStandards: trace every major claim
explicitly mark human confirmations

roles.purpose: Hold the plan to stated scope, time, and reversibility constraints.
roles.goal: Prevent the plan from quietly expanding beyond the human's real constraints.
instructions: Map revisions back to explicit constraints.
Push toward reversible, staged plans.
Flag steps that assume missing time, budget, or permissions.
mergeRules: Prefer smaller sharper scope when confidence is low.
Flag steps that violate explicit constraints instead of rewriting around them silently.
evidenceStandards: cite explicit constraints
surface tradeoffs

roles.purpose: Represent what the human is actually trying to optimize for.
roles.goal: Reduce plan drift from the user's priorities and success criteria.
instructions: Prefer the human's likely priorities over elegant abstractions.
Isolate choices that need human arbitration.
Identify when the plan answers a different question than the one asked.
mergeRules: Prefer the stated user goal over elegant but off-target abstractions.
Convert unresolved preference tradeoffs into explicit human decisions.
evidenceStandards: ground in explicit user language where available

roles.purpose: Merge accepted revisions into a clean packet while preserving dissent.
roles.goal: Produce a trustworthy, decision-ready planning packet rather than a pile of comments.
instructions: Incorporate accepted revisions without erasing objections.
Keep required human decisions explicit and few.
Prefer clarity and legitimacy over verbosity.
mergeRules: Preserve dissent instead of smoothing it away.
Merge only accepted revisions; unresolved blockers stay visible.
evidenceStandards: carry forward evidence labels and rationale

roles.purpose: Protect trust boundaries, auth, and sensitive data handling.
roles.goal: Ensure security-sensitive plans surface validation gates before execution work begins.
instructions: Flag auth, privilege, secret, and untrusted-input issues early.
Require clear validation gates for sensitive paths.
Escalate critical unknowns to explicit human decisions.
mergeRules: Never clear a security blocker without an explicit gate.
Prefer narrow reversible steps on sensitive paths.
evidenceStandards: tie claims to affected trust boundary

roles.purpose: Protect data provenance, handling, and least-necessary retention.
roles.goal: Stop planning packets from glossing over data-handling implications.
instructions: Check where data enters, moves, persists, and is reused.
Flag unclear provenance or retention assumptions.
Require human confirmation for ambiguous sensitive data use.
mergeRules: Push ambiguous sensitive-data handling into explicit human confirmation.
Do not imply data provenance that is not evidenced.
evidenceStandards: label unknown data handling explicitly

roles.purpose: Stress-test failure paths, rollback, and partial completion.
roles.goal: Ensure the plan is survivable when dependencies fail or work stops halfway.
instructions: Enumerate failure modes and early warning signals.
Require rollback or recovery guidance when reversibility is not straightforward.
Prefer gated rollout over one-shot plans.
mergeRules: Require rollback or recovery guidance when reversibility is not straightforward.
Prefer staged validation over one-shot rollout.
evidenceStandards: tie failure modes to real dependency or state transitions

roles.purpose: Represent operator load, runbook clarity, and serviceability.
roles.goal: Make sure the plan can be run and supported by real operators.
instructions: Look for manual toil and handoff gaps.
Require clear validation and rollback checkpoints.
Surface where support and ops need prep before rollout.
mergeRules: Surface missing ownership early.
Prefer serviceable runbook checkpoints over clever hidden automation.
evidenceStandards: tie issues to real operating surface

roles.purpose: Protect budget realism and prevent overbuilt plans.
roles.goal: Force plans to confront cost, sequencing, and opportunity-cost tradeoffs.
instructions: Prefer smaller sharp scope when confidence is low.
Flag hidden cost multipliers and payment touchpoints.
Require explicit human decisions when cost tradeoffs are unresolved.
mergeRules: Prefer lower-blast-radius first slices when cost confidence is weak.
Escalate unresolved spend tradeoffs into human decisions.
evidenceStandards: state where cost is estimated vs unknown

roles.purpose: Protect policy and approval legitimacy for sensitive plans.
roles.goal: Ensure plans surface required human or policy approvals before implementation.
instructions: Identify required approvals and policy checkpoints.
Escalate unresolved legitimacy questions instead of smoothing them over.
Keep compliance review narrow and explicit.
mergeRules: List required approvals explicitly.
Do not mark ready while legitimacy gaps remain implicit.
evidenceStandards: explicitly identify what needs human approval

roles.goal: Prevent customer-facing plans from underestimating confusion, friction, or messaging needs.
instructions: Look for user-facing failure modes and support burden.
Require rollout messaging or support prep when impact is material.
Flag wording that may mislead downstream teams.
mergeRules: Expose customer-facing fallout before implementation starts.
Require support readiness notes when customer impact is material.
evidenceStandards: tie concern to expected customer change

roles.purpose: Stress plan seams between systems, teams, and contracts.
roles.goal: Make dependency and compatibility assumptions explicit.
instructions: Enumerate non-local dependencies and the order they constrain.
Flag missing owners and missing interface contracts.
Prefer staged validation over big-bang coordination.
mergeRules: Name cross-system owners explicitly.
Prefer staged validation when contracts are uncertain.
evidenceStandards: tie dependency risk to named system or owner

roles.purpose: Protect shared platform and infrastructure surfaces.
roles.goal: Ensure local plans do not quietly impose global platform risk.
instructions: Check shared-resource, migration, and deployment assumptions.
Push for reversible rollout and clear ownership.
Flag broad blast-radius changes early.
mergeRules: Call out shared-surface risk early.
Prefer reversible rollout when the plan touches platform edges.
evidenceStandards: name the shared surface or migration edge

roles.purpose: Inject domain-specific nuance when the general council may miss it.
roles.goal: Reduce generic-plan error for novel or specialized work.
instructions: Call out domain assumptions that would fail in practice.
Prefer narrow, domain-grounded objections.
Avoid broad rewrites unless domain correctness requires it.
mergeRules: Avoid generic rewrites when domain nuance matters.
Only push broad plan changes when domain correctness requires them.
evidenceStandards: tie objection to domain fact or missing fact

roles.purpose: Represent abuse resistance and community harm surfaces.
roles.goal: Ensure plans with community or safety implications expose abuse-path thinking before execution.
instructions: Look for harm amplification, moderation gaps, and unsafe defaults.
Escalate when abuse resistance depends on unknown downstream behavior.
Prefer clear human checkpoints for risky safety decisions.
mergeRules: Keep unsafe defaults visible.
Escalate uncertain abuse-resistance assumptions into human checkpoints.
evidenceStandards: tie concern to concrete abuse or harm surface
